label: SES-049 conversation entity schema designed: 1 session, 6 decisions (DEC-129..134), 0 planning items, 7 references

session.title: Conversation entity schema designed; second per-entity governance schema specification produced
session.conversation_reference: Claude.ai schema-design conversation for the conversation entity — second per-entity schema-design conversation in the governance entity schema-design workstream. Opened against PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/schema-design-kickoff-conversation.md (the kickoff committed as part of SES-047's artifacts at 369f32e on 05-20-26). The conversation produced one deliverable per the kickoff and the schema-spec methodology guide's template: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/governance-schema-specs/conversation.md (459 lines, committed at 6580a32). Architectural discussion was driven by the operating preferences for ARCHITECTURE mode (surface only decisions that pass the two-part test of real downstream impact and meaningfully different viable options; decide routine items as drafts; consequential decisions use the eight-element template). Four consequential decisions were surfaced and resolved via the template; eleven routine items were drafted and confirmed as a single batch after Doug instructed Claude to proceed end-to-end without per-decision confirmation. The conversation also surfaced one pre-existing drift between the kickoff prompt's working assumptions and the cross-spec precedent locked by SES-048's workstream.md — resolved in favour of the precedent (references-edge over foreign-key for parent-child governance relationships). No transcript preserved per DEC-025.
session.topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Design the `conversation` entity type schema for V2's storage layer. Drive a structured architectural discussion that produces one deliverable: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/governance-schema-specs/conversation.md — the complete schema specification per the template in governance-entity-schema-spec-guide.md. Cadence matches the methodology workstream's schema-design conversations and the user-interface planning conversations: structured architectural discussion driven one decision at a time, building toward the specification section by section. At conversation close: decisions written via direct API as decision records; any deferred items written as planning items; one session record written by Doug through the V2 desktop New Session dialog per the session-record-at-close pattern."

Topics worked through:

1. Pre-flight orientation. Sparse-cloned the crmbuilder repo and read the eight-item reading list named in the kickoff: crmbuilder/CLAUDE.md, governance-schema-workstream-plan.md, governance-entity-schema-spec-guide.md, governance-schema-specs/workstream.md (the just-completed predecessor specification), the six foundation decisions DEC-117..122 plus the six SES-048 decisions DEC-123..128 from db-export/decisions.json, SES-046, SES-047, and SES-048 from db-export/sessions.json, crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/access/vocab.py, and methodology-schema-specs/domain.md as the structural exemplar. API health check (against the conversation's sandbox localhost, not Doug's machine) and pytest run (sparse checkout doesn't include tests/) were flagged as not meaningfully runnable in the conversation environment per the SES-048 precedent; design-only conversation, so neither gates.

2. Cross-spec precedents inherited from SES-048 surfaced at orientation: references-edge over foreign-key for parent-child governance relationships, per-status lifecycle timestamps for workflow-shaped lifecycles, and terminal-states-are-terminal discipline. Items already pre-committed by workstream.md and not re-decided by this conversation: the kind `conversation_belongs_to_workstream` (its name and direction were settled in workstream.md; this conversation registers it in vocab.py but does not re-decide it); ENTITY_TYPES additions `conversation` and `reference_book` (already listed in workstream.md's additions table).

3. Drift surfaced before opening Decision 1. The kickoff's working assumption for the conversation-to-session linkage was 'add a conversation_id foreign key to session in the build (back-fillable for historical records), keep direct relationship simple.' But workstream.md locked references-edge as the cross-spec precedent for parent-child governance relationships, and the conversation-to-session relationship is structurally parent-child (a conversation produces zero or one sessions per DEC-013). The drift is exactly analogous to the SES-048 drift on the workstream-to-conversation relationship; the locked precedent binds unless clear cause to deviate. The kickoff's explicit 'do NOT modify sessions table shape' boundary reinforces the precedent — the FK alternative would modify the append-only sessions table per DEC-013, the references-edge alternative does not. No other drifts.

4. Decision 1 — conversation-to-session relationship mechanism. Eight-element template. Resolved in favour of references-edge per the SES-048 precedent. New relationship kind named: conversation_records_session (source=conversation, target=session, source-first per DEC-048, past-tense 'records' mirrors workstream_planned_in_reference_book's past-participle posture). Cardinality at-most-one outgoing per conversation per DEC-013. Required-when rule: edge required when conversation_status='complete', enforced at the access layer with the same combined-validation pattern as workstream's supersession-requires-edge rule (HTTP 422 with {error: complete_conversation_requires_session_edge}). Approved Option A (references-edge).

5. Decision 2 — lifecycle granularity. Eight-element template. Resolved to keep all seven states from DEC-119 in full (planned → kickoff_drafted → ready → in_flight → one of complete / cancelled / superseded). The kickoff_drafted-vs-ready distinction is operationally real and was demonstrated by the project's own conversation lineage at the kickoff's authoring time: workstream's kickoff was drafted and ready, conversation's kickoff was drafted but not ready (waiting on workstream to close), reference_book's kickoff was drafted but two conversations away from openable. Collapsing the two states would lose the operationally meaningful distinction. Inherited truly-terminal posture from workstream's precedent (no transitions out of complete/cancelled/superseded, including between terminal states). Added the complete-requires-session-edge rule per Decision 1; supersession-requires-edge rule inherited verbatim from workstream. Forward-only planning lifecycle (no regression within the four non-terminal states either). Six per-status lifecycle timestamps named (one per non-starter status; planned_at collapses into created_at per workstream precedent): conversation_kickoff_drafted_at, conversation_ready_at, conversation_started_at, conversation_completed_at, conversation_cancelled_at, conversation_superseded_at. Approved Option A (full seven states).

6. Decision 3 — conversation-to-kickoff (work_ticket) relationship. Eight-element template. Resolved to references-edge with tentative new kind conversation_opens_against_work_ticket (active verb, present tense, source=conversation). Cardinality at-most-one outgoing per conversation. Required-when rule: edge required when conversation_status is in {kickoff_drafted, ready, in_flight, complete}; optional for {planned, cancelled, superseded}. Kind-name posture: committed tentatively here parallel to workstream.md's tentative workstream_planned_in_reference_book commitment; the work_ticket schema-design conversation may refine for verb-tense consistency with work_ticket's other inbound kinds; build-planning reconciles. ENTITY_TYPES gains work_ticket. Approved Option A.

7. Decision 4 — predecessor/successor chaining between conversations. Eight-element template. Resolved to introduce new kind conversation_succeeds_conversation (source=successor, target=predecessor, source-first per DEC-048). Diverges from workstream.md's defer-sequencing posture on the strength of conversation-sequencing frequency: every conversation in a sequenced workstream has predecessors and successors, captured today only in in_flight_at_end fields of session records (the weak-coupling pattern DEC-121 says to eliminate), while workstream sequencing is comparatively rare. Cardinality many-to-many (a build-planning conversation has six predecessor schema-design conversations; a workstream-establishing conversation has multiple direct successors). No required-when rule. Cycle prevention deferred to build-planning. New cross-spec precedent established: typed sequencing edges are introduced when entity-family frequency justifies, distinct from workstream's defer-sequencing posture. The remaining four schemas inherit the precedent and may deviate with rationale. Approved Option A.

8. After Decision 4 Doug instructed Claude to 'Proceed with making your recommendations final decision and complete the prompt without asking for further input.' Routine items 5-15 from the proposed sequence were resolved as a single batch and folded directly into the spec draft.

9. Routine items resolved without per-decision confirmation: (a) identifier prefix CONV, four letters matching PROC/CRMC precedent; (b) field inventory minimum-viable — no estimated_duration, no priority, no conversation_kind, intended-deliverable description folded into conversation_description; (c) workstream membership mandatory at the access layer, enforced as 'exactly one outgoing conversation_belongs_to_workstream edge'; (d) per-status lifecycle timestamps as enumerated in Decision 2; (e) cross-workstream conversation references use existing generic references/is_about kinds, no new vocabulary; (f) soft-delete with restore matching workstream's posture, distinct from cancelled status; (g) standard 8-endpoint API surface; (h) UI deviations — master pane gains a Workstream column as the fifth column (vs workstream's four), plus workstream_identifier and status filter combos in the panel toolbar realising the new query-string parameters on the list endpoint; (i) eighteen acceptance criteria (parallel to workstream's sixteen, with two added for the workstream-membership-required rule and the new list-endpoint filters); (j) vocabulary additions named in the spec table for build-planning aggregation; (k) cross-references section mirrors workstream.md's structure with the additional six SES-048 decisions cited as related prior decisions.

10. Spec drafting in one pass per spec guide section 4.3. governance-schema-specs/conversation.md drafted following the structural shape of governance-schema-specs/workstream.md, with all nine required subsections of section 3 substantively filled. Eighteen testable acceptance criteria captured (spec guide gate requires 10). Section 3.8 categorized by build / backfill / future tags per the spec guide convention with five build items (sidebar grouping, migration ordering, tentative kind names on both outbound and inbound sides, backfill behavior on create with terminal status, cycle prevention in conversation_succeeds_conversation), five backfill items inheriting from PI-022 plus new items specific to this entity (historical lifecycle timestamps, status assignment for prior conversations, predecessor-successor edges from in_flight_at_end text, kickoff-to-work_ticket edges from seed prompts, mandatory-workstream-membership for ad-hoc conversations like SES-046), and five future items (estimated duration, priority, conversation kind/type, outcome summary, pause/resume states, cross-workstream typed kinds). Committed at 6580a32 in one commit.

11. Close-out artifacts authored. After spec commit, this payload (ses_049.json) and the companion apply prompt (CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-049.md) were drafted in one pass. Identifier check at close-out: latest applied SES is SES-048 so SES-049 is next-available; latest DEC is DEC-128 so DEC-129..134 is next-available for the six decisions; no new planning items (PI-022 covers retroactive backfill including this conversation's records).
session.artifacts_produced:
One file committed to PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ in the crmbuilder repo during the conversation, plus two more produced at close:

During the conversation:

(1) governance-schema-specs/conversation.md (new, v1.0, 459 lines, committed at 6580a32). Second per-entity schema specification in the governance entity schema-design workstream. Conforms to governance-entity-schema-spec-guide.md. Establishes the conversation entity type for V2 storage with six content/classification fields plus nine timestamps (three inherited base + six per-status lifecycle), seven-status lifecycle (planned → kickoff_drafted → ready → in_flight → one of complete / cancelled / superseded) with truly terminal terminals inherited from workstream's precedent plus a forward-only planning lifecycle and two access-layer rules (complete-requires-session-edge, supersession-requires-edge). No foreign-key columns (every relationship in refs per Decision 1 and the inherited precedent). Four references-edge kinds registered: conversation_belongs_to_workstream (declared inbound in workstream.md, registered here per the deferral note), conversation_records_session (new), conversation_opens_against_work_ticket (new, tentative), conversation_succeeds_conversation (new, establishing the typed-sequencing precedent). Standard 8-endpoint API surface with two new list-endpoint filters (workstream_identifier, status). Default UI layout with a fifth master-pane column (Workstream) and matching toolbar filter combos. Eighteen testable acceptance criteria captured. Vocabulary additions named for build-planning aggregation: REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS += {conversation_belongs_to_workstream, conversation_records_session, conversation_opens_against_work_ticket, conversation_succeeds_conversation}; ENTITY_TYPES += {conversation, work_ticket}; four _kinds_for_pair clauses; matching Alembic migration on refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint.

At conversation close:

(2) close-out-payloads/ses_049.json (new — this payload). One session (SES-049), six decisions (DEC-129..134), zero planning items, seven references (six decided_in plus one is_about SES-049 → PI-022).

(3) prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-049.md (new — this payload's apply prompt). Idempotent apply targeting the CRMBUILDER engagement.

Via this payload's apply prompt, the following records land in the CRMBUILDER engagement database:

(4) DEC-129 — conversation identifier prefix CONV, four-letter form matching PROC/CRMC precedent.

(5) DEC-130 — conversation-to-session via references-table edge with new kind conversation_records_session per the inherited cross-spec precedent from DEC-124; resolves the kickoff prompt's drift toward an FK column on the sessions table in favour of the precedent; preserves the append-only sessions table shape per DEC-013.

(6) DEC-131 — conversation lifecycle: seven statuses with truly terminal terminals, forward-only planning lifecycle, complete-requires-session-edge rule, supersession-requires-edge rule inherited from workstream verbatim.

(7) DEC-132 — conversation-to-kickoff (work_ticket) via references-table edge with tentative new kind conversation_opens_against_work_ticket; required-when rule binds the edge to statuses past kickoff_drafted; work_ticket conversation may refine; build-planning reconciles.

(8) DEC-133 — conversation_succeeds_conversation introduced for predecessor-successor chaining within a workstream; diverges from workstream's defer-sequencing posture on frequency grounds; establishes new cross-spec precedent that typed sequencing edges are introduced when entity-family frequency justifies.

(9) DEC-134 — conversation field inventory (six content/classification fields plus nine timestamps); list-endpoint filters for workstream_identifier and status; master-pane Workstream column as fifth column; default soft-delete with restore distinct from cancelled status; eighteen acceptance criteria captured.

(10) Six decided_in references (DEC-129..134 → SES-049) plus one is_about reference (SES-049 → PI-022, mirroring SES-047 and SES-048's pattern of linking the schema-design conversation to the retroactive-backfill planning item).

No source code modified — this is a planning conversation producing schema specifications only. Vocabulary changes, schema migrations, and access-layer methods are aggregated by the build-planning conversation after all six schemas exist.
session.in_flight_at_end: Next workstream conversation: reference book entity schema design (third per-entity schema-design conversation, of six). Opens against PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/schema-design-kickoff-reference-book.md. That conversation produces governance-schema-specs/reference_book.md and inherits the four cross-spec precedents now in force: the three from workstream (references-edge over foreign-key for parent-child governance relationships, per-status lifecycle timestamps for workflow-shaped lifecycles, terminal-states-are-terminal discipline) plus the one new precedent from this conversation (typed sequencing edges introduced when entity-family frequency justifies, distinct from workstream's defer-sequencing posture). The reference_book entity is structurally most directly related to workstream through the master-plan linkage kind already declared in workstream.md (workstream_planned_in_reference_book); the reference_book conversation may refine that kind name for consistency with reference_book's other inbound kinds, and build-planning reconciles. After reference_book.md lands, three more schema-design conversations follow in workstream order (work_ticket, close_out_payload, deposit_event). After all six schema specifications exist, the build-planning conversation opens against governance-schema-build-planning-kickoff.md (to be drafted at the close of the deposit-event conversation per SES-047's in-flight notes) and produces the integrating Product Requirements Document, implementation plan, per-slice build prompts, and the consolidated vocab.py update aggregating relationship-kind additions from all six specs. Multi-tenancy routing fix slice B and the Cleveland Business Mentors redo Phase 1 conversation continue in parallel as independent workstreams per DEC-122.

decisions.title: Conversation entity identifier prefix and format: CONV, four-letter form matching PROC and CRMC precedents
decisions.context: The conversation entity is the second of six new governance entities designed in the governance entity schema-design workstream. Its identifier prefix sets a small precedent for the cross-spec collision list. DEC-123 affirmed that the cross-spec norm is not locked at two letters; each downstream conversation makes its own call within the spec guide's 2-to-5-letter range. The kickoff prompt named CONV as a working assumption with CONVO, CHAT, and CV as alternatives. The existing collision list spans two-letter (PI, WS), three-letter (DEC, SES, RSK, TOP, REF, CHR, STA, DOM, ENT), and four-letter (PROC, CRMC) prefixes.
decisions.decision: **Adopt `CONV` as the conversation identifier prefix, format `CONV-NNN` zero-padded to three digits, server-assigned on POST omission with helper at GET /conversations/next-identifier.** The four-letter form matches the existing PROC and CRMC methodology-entity precedents and avoids the ambiguity of two-letter CV. Spec guide section 6's collision list is extended with CONV.
decisions.rationale: CONV is mnemonic, easy to scan, and unambiguous. The four-letter form aligns with the methodology-entity precedents (PROC for process, CRMC for crm_candidate). Two-letter CV could read as 'curriculum vitae' or other domain shorthand; the longer form removes ambiguity. CONVO and CHAT were considered (the kickoff named both as alternatives); CONVO is one letter longer for no semantic gain; CHAT changes the term of art from 'conversation' (the project's working language) to a different word.
decisions.alternatives_considered: **Option (b) — CV.** Two letters, matches PI and WS short-prefix posture. Rejected because the abbreviation reads as 'curriculum vitae' or other domain shorthand and reduces scannability. **Option (c) — CONVO.** Five letters, no semantic improvement over CONV. Rejected because the longer form adds no value. **Option (d) — CHAT.** Four letters, changes the term of art from the project's working language. Rejected because the entity is documented throughout the project's writing as 'conversation,' and the prefix should match.
decisions.consequences: Spec guide section 6 collision list extended with CONV. The remaining four governance entity conversations (reference book, work ticket, close-out payload, deposit event) have first refusal on their own prefix choices, with no cross-spec length norm locked at this conversation. The conversation entity's spec section 3.1 documents the four-letter posture explicitly.

decisions.title: Conversation-to-session relationship via references-table edge per the SES-048 precedent, with new relationship kind conversation_records_session
decisions.context: The kickoff prompt for SES-049 carried a working assumption that the conversation-to-session relationship would be a direct foreign-key column on the sessions table (conversation_identifier, back-fillable for historical records, with the kickoff calling the approach 'keep direct relationship simple'). SES-048 locked the cross-spec precedent of references-edge over foreign-key for parent-child governance relationships (DEC-124). The conversation-to-session relationship is structurally parent-child — a conversation produces zero or one sessions per DEC-013. The kickoff's own 'What this conversation does NOT do' section explicitly forbids modifying the existing sessions table shape, reinforcing the precedent's applicability. The drift between the kickoff's FK working assumption and the locked precedent is exactly analogous to the SES-048 drift on the workstream-to-conversation relationship.
decisions.decision: **Adopt the references-table edge mechanism per the SES-048 cross-spec precedent (DEC-124).** The conversation-to-session relationship is modelled as a references-table edge with new relationship kind `conversation_records_session` (source-first per DEC-048: source = conversation, target = session, past-tense 'records' mirrors workstream_planned_in_reference_book's past-participle posture). No `conversation_identifier` column on the sessions table; no modification to the append-only sessions table shape per DEC-013. Cardinality: at most one outgoing edge per conversation. Required-when rule: edge required when conversation_status = 'complete', enforced at the access layer with the same combined-validation pattern as workstream's supersession-requires-edge rule (HTTP 422 with {error: complete_conversation_requires_session_edge}). Vocabulary additions: REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS += conversation_records_session; matching clause in _kinds_for_pair for (conversation, session).
decisions.rationale: The SES-048 precedent is binding — cross-spec precedents are overridden only with clear cause, and the kickoff's preference for foreign-key offered convenience rather than cause. The references-edge posture matches the governance entity family's coherent architectural shape: parent-child relationships across the six new entities all use the same mechanism. The kickoff's own 'do NOT modify sessions table shape' boundary reinforces the choice — the FK alternative would have modified the append-only sessions table per DEC-013, requiring an Alembic migration and a backfill pass to populate historical rows; the references-edge alternative does not touch the sessions table at all. The query and schema-enforcement costs of references-edge over foreign-key are real but small at expected scale (50+ existing conversations, ~10 new per workstream).
decisions.alternatives_considered: **Option (b) — Foreign-key column `conversation_identifier` on the sessions table (the kickoff's working assumption).** Would give a single indexed filter for 'session for conversation X' and column-level constraint enforcement of the at-most-one-session-per-conversation rule. Rejected because the SES-048 precedent explicitly chose references-edge for analogous parent-child relationships, the kickoff itself forbade modifying the sessions table shape, and DEC-013 establishes sessions as the most delicate table in the governance system (modifying its shape requires strong justification). **Option (c) — Computed via text matching only (no formal relationship; rely on session.conversation_reference descriptive text per DEC-025).** Rejected because DEC-121's coverage extension principle requires machine-resolvable linkages between governance entities; text matching is exactly the weak-coupling pattern DEC-121 says to eliminate.
decisions.consequences: One new relationship_kind to register in vocab.py (conversation_records_session); matching Alembic migration on refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint. Access-layer rule enforcing at-most-one session edge per conversation plus required-when-status-equals-complete rule; tests required for both. The sessions table shape is unmodified; no migration on sessions; no backfill column populate. The references-edge precedent locked by SES-048 is reinforced for the four remaining governance entity schema-design conversations. PI-022's backfill pass reconstructs the conversation_records_session edges for historical conversations from the descriptive text in session.conversation_reference.

decisions.title: Conversation lifecycle: seven statuses with truly terminal terminals, forward-only planning lifecycle, complete-requires-session-edge and supersession-requires-edge rules
decisions.context: DEC-119 names seven working statuses for the conversation entity: planned, kickoff_drafted, ready, in_flight, complete, cancelled, superseded. The kickoff prompt for SES-049 asked whether all seven were needed in the user-interface version 0 build, specifically whether kickoff_drafted and ready could collapse into one state. The conversation also needed to decide the truly-terminal posture (SES-048 locked terminal-states-are-terminal for workstream; conversation inherits the precedent by default), the transition map within the four non-terminal states, and the access-layer rules tied to specific statuses (the conversation→session edge from Decision 1 implies a required-when rule analogous to workstream's supersession-requires-edge rule).
decisions.decision: **Adopt the seven-status set from DEC-119 in full, with the following transition map and access-layer rules.** (a) Status values: planned (default starter), kickoff_drafted, ready, in_flight, complete (terminal), cancelled (terminal), superseded (terminal). (b) Forward-only planning lifecycle: the four non-terminal states form a strict line (planned → kickoff_drafted → ready → in_flight), with no regressive transitions. cancelled and superseded reachable from any non-terminal state. (c) Truly-terminal posture inherited from workstream's DEC-125 precedent: terminal states reject all outgoing transitions including between terminal states. (d) Complete-requires-session-edge rule: setting status to complete requires an outgoing conversation_records_session edge per Decision 1 (DEC-130); HTTP 422 with {error: complete_conversation_requires_session_edge} on violation. (e) Supersession-requires-edge rule: inherited verbatim from workstream's DEC-125; setting status to superseded requires an outgoing supersedes edge to another conversation record. (f) Six per-status lifecycle timestamps (one per non-starter status; planned_at collapses into created_at per workstream precedent): conversation_kickoff_drafted_at, conversation_ready_at, conversation_started_at, conversation_completed_at, conversation_cancelled_at, conversation_superseded_at. Server-set on transition; three terminal timestamps mutually exclusive at the terminal level.
decisions.rationale: The kickoff_drafted-vs-ready distinction is operationally real and was demonstrated by the project's own conversation lineage at the kickoff's authoring time: workstream's kickoff was drafted and ready, conversation's kickoff was drafted but not ready (waiting on workstream to close), reference_book's kickoff was drafted but two conversations away from openable. Collapsing the two states would lose this operationally meaningful distinction or require a separate computed-readiness signal to recover it. The methodology guide explicitly admits 'Specifications with more than three statuses are expected and do not need special justification, provided the states are operationally meaningful and transitions are well-defined' (section 5). The forward-only planning lifecycle preserves timeline semantics — a conversation that 'regresses' from ready back to kickoff_drafted because an upstream dependency surfaced is conceptually a new conversation (cancel and re-plan, or delay the actual opening). The truly-terminal posture matches workstream's. The complete-requires-session-edge rule realizes Decision 1's references-edge mechanism — a complete conversation without a session edge is in an inconsistent state. The six per-status lifecycle timestamps follow DEC-126's per-status timestamps precedent extended from workstream's four columns to conversation's six (more lifecycle states means more timestamps).
decisions.alternatives_considered: **Option (b) — Collapse kickoff_drafted and ready into a single state.** Would reduce the status set from seven to six. Rejected because the operational distinction (kickoff exists but not openable vs. kickoff exists and dependencies met) is real and frequent. **Option (c) — Add a paused/hold status for in-flight conversations awaiting external input.** Rejected because no real operational signal supports it at the time of this conversation; deferred to future-release per spec section 3.8.3 parallel to workstream's deferred pause/resume posture. **Option (d) — Allow regression within the planning lifecycle.** Rejected because timeline semantics are clearer with forward-only; the cost of cancelling and re-planning a conversation when scope changes is real but small.
decisions.consequences: Conversations table has 15 columns (6 substantive + 3 inherited base + 6 per-status lifecycle). Server-side enforcement of forward-only transition map plus truly-terminal posture plus complete-requires-session-edge plus supersession-requires-edge; four access-layer rules to test. Future-release additions (estimated duration, priority, conversation kind, outcome summary, paused/hold states, cross-workstream typed kinds) are documented as deferred in spec section 3.8.3. PI-022's backfill pass populates the six lifecycle timestamp columns for historical conversation records; the backfill policy for which historical dates to use is PI-022's call.

decisions.title: Conversation-to-kickoff (work_ticket) relationship via references-table edge with tentative new kind conversation_opens_against_work_ticket
decisions.context: Every conversation has one kickoff prompt — captured as a work_ticket record per the workstream plan's single-use-seed-document framing. The work_ticket entity is the fourth schema-design conversation in the workstream and has not yet been designed at SES-049's writing. The conversation entity's spec must declare the relationship from conversation to work_ticket; the question is the mechanism (references-edge per the precedent, or foreign-key column on conversation as a deviation) and the kind name (which the work_ticket conversation may refine for consistency with work_ticket's other inbound kinds).
decisions.decision: **Adopt references-edge with tentative new kind `conversation_opens_against_work_ticket`.** Source = conversation, target = work_ticket. Active verb 'opens against' mirrors how Doug refers to kickoffs in practice ('Opens against PRDs/.../schema-design-kickoff-X.md') and reads as present-tense ongoing relationship between the conversation and its kickoff. Cardinality: at most one outgoing edge per conversation; exactly one inbound edge per work_ticket (the work_ticket's single-use property is enforced from work_ticket's side). Required-when rule: edge required when conversation_status is in {kickoff_drafted, ready, in_flight, complete}; optional for {planned, cancelled, superseded}. Kind name is tentative — the work_ticket schema-design conversation may refine for verb-tense consistency with work_ticket's other inbound kinds (for example, if work_ticket ends up with a uniform consumed-by verb pattern, this kind might shift to conversation_consumes_work_ticket); build-planning reconciles. ENTITY_TYPES gains work_ticket. Vocabulary additions: REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS += conversation_opens_against_work_ticket; ENTITY_TYPES += work_ticket; matching clause in _kinds_for_pair for (conversation, work_ticket).
decisions.rationale: References-edge inherited from the SES-048 cross-spec precedent (DEC-124) — the conversation-to-work_ticket relationship is structurally parent-child (conversation outlives work_ticket; work_ticket is consumed during one conversation), and parent-child governance relationships use references-edge across the family. Active verb 'opens against' reads naturally in the project's writing context (Doug's commit messages, session records, and kickoff prompts uniformly use this phrasing). Past-participle alternatives (conversation_seeded_by_work_ticket) read more passively and put the work_ticket in the verb role; given conversation is the active organising unit, the source-active framing reads better. Required-when rule mirrors the complete-requires-session-edge rule from DEC-131 and the supersession-requires-edge rule from DEC-125 — the access layer enforces status-relationship consistency uniformly across the conversation entity. Tentative kind-name posture parallels workstream.md's tentative workstream_planned_in_reference_book commitment; build-planning has explicit reconciliation responsibility per the spec guide section 6 cross-spec consistency check.
decisions.alternatives_considered: **Option (b) — Foreign-key column work_ticket_id on conversation.** Rejected per the inherited references-edge precedent from DEC-124. **Option (c) — Foreign-key column conversation_id on work_ticket.** Would model the relationship from work_ticket's side. Rejected for the same precedent reason and because it would commit a column on a not-yet-designed table from this spec's side, which the work_ticket conversation could not change without breaking this spec. **Option (d) — Past-participle kind name (conversation_seeded_by_work_ticket).** Rejected because the active-verb framing matches the project's working language and the source-first convention reads more naturally with the active verb. **Option (e) — Defer the relationship entirely to the work_ticket conversation.** Rejected because the conversation entity's spec must describe its outbound relationships to be considered complete per spec guide section 2; deferring the relationship would leave the conversation entity's spec incomplete.
decisions.consequences: One new relationship_kind to register in vocab.py (conversation_opens_against_work_ticket); one new ENTITY_TYPES entry (work_ticket); matching Alembic migration on refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint and the ENTITY_TYPES enumeration. Access-layer rule enforcing at-most-one work_ticket edge per conversation plus required-when rule binding the edge to statuses past kickoff_drafted. The work_ticket schema-design conversation (third after this one) may refine the kind name; build-planning reconciles. PI-022's backfill pass reconstructs conversation_opens_against_work_ticket edges from the kickoff-prompt filenames named in historical session records' seed prompts and topics_covered text.

decisions.title: conversation_succeeds_conversation introduced for predecessor-successor chaining; new cross-spec precedent that typed sequencing edges are introduced when entity-family frequency justifies
decisions.context: Conversations within a workstream are often sequenced. This conversation's kickoff names a predecessor (the workstream schema-design conversation, SES-048) and a successor (the reference book schema-design conversation, planned). In a strictly sequenced workstream like the governance schema-design workstream, every conversation has explicit predecessor and successor relationships. In other workstreams (like the user-interface version 0.5 engagement-management workstream), conversations may be parallel — the workstream-establishing conversation has multiple direct successors. The chaining is captured today only in the in_flight_at_end fields of session records (free text describing what comes next); DEC-121's coverage extension principle says machine-resolvable linkages should replace free-text correspondence. The question for this conversation: does the schema introduce a typed sequencing kind in this release, or defer to signal as workstream.md did for its parallel workstream_succeeds_workstream question (section 3.8.3 of workstream.md)?
decisions.decision: **Introduce new relationship kind `conversation_succeeds_conversation` in this release.** Source = successor conversation, target = predecessor conversation, source-first per DEC-048. Cardinality many-to-many (a build-planning conversation has six predecessor schema-design conversations; a workstream-establishing conversation has multiple direct successors). No required-when rule — chaining is optional, captured when meaningfully sequenced. Cycle prevention deferred to build-planning (the working assumption is access-layer DFS at insert time; the error response is HTTP 422 with {error: circular_conversation_succession}). Diverges from workstream's defer-sequencing posture on the strength of frequency. **Establishes new cross-spec precedent: typed sequencing edges are introduced when the entity family's sequencing is structurally frequent.** The remaining four governance entity schemas (reference_book, work_ticket, close_out_payload, deposit_event) inherit the precedent and may introduce parallel typed sequencing edges of their own, or defer, based on the same frequency test. Vocabulary additions: REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS += conversation_succeeds_conversation; matching clause in _kinds_for_pair for (conversation, conversation) — note that the existing _kinds_for_pair rule 'if source_type == target_type: kinds.add(supersedes)' already admits supersedes for this pair; the new clause adds conversation_succeeds_conversation alongside without conflict.
decisions.rationale: Conversation sequencing is structurally part of every conversation's existence within a workstream — every conversation in a sequenced workstream has predecessors and successors. The existing fallback (filter conversations by workstream_id and status=planned, sort by planned_at) works for strict sequences but fails when a workstream-establishing conversation has multiple parallel successors. The text-matching alternative (parse in_flight_at_end fields to find named-conversation references) is exactly the weak-coupling pattern DEC-121 says to eliminate. Workstream's defer-sequencing posture is appropriate at workstream-entity granularity because workstream sequencing is rare (most workstreams are independent of each other); the frequency argument inverts at conversation granularity. The supersedes kind and the conversation_succeeds_conversation kind capture distinct semantics — supersedes is a directed replacement (one conversation abandoned, another carries forward, source's lifecycle terminates at superseded), while conversation_succeeds_conversation is a directed continuation (both conversations exist as healthy lifecycle objects, source positioned after target in planned sequence). The two kinds never co-occur on the same source-target pair.
decisions.alternatives_considered: **Option (b) — Defer to signal, parallel to workstream.md's posture.** Rejected because conversation sequencing is observed today on every conversation in this workstream; the signal is already present, not pending. **Option (c) — Foreign-key column predecessor_conversation_id on conversation.** Rejected per the inherited references-edge precedent from DEC-124 and because the many-to-many cardinality (a conversation may have multiple predecessors) does not fit a single-column FK. **Option (d) — Reuse supersedes for both sequencing and supersession.** Rejected because the semantics are distinct — supersession terminates the source's lifecycle, succession does not. **Option (e) — Introduce the kind but without a cycle-prevention rule.** Rejected because circular succession (A succeeds B which succeeds A) is semantically meaningless and would create graph-traversal hazards in any query that walks the DAG; cycle prevention is required, with the specific mechanism deferred to build-planning.
decisions.consequences: One new relationship_kind to register in vocab.py (conversation_succeeds_conversation); matching Alembic migration on refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint. Access-layer cycle-prevention enforcement at insert time; tests required. The new cross-spec precedent on typed-sequencing-frequency-justification is documented in the spec's section 1 and 3.3.1 and informs the four remaining schema-design conversations: reference_book may have version-sequencing semantics that justify a typed kind, work_ticket has single-use semantics that argue against any sequencing, close_out_payload is produced once per conversation (no sequencing), deposit_event has retry semantics that may justify a typed retry-sequencing kind. Each downstream conversation applies the frequency test. PI-022's backfill pass reconstructs conversation_succeeds_conversation edges from in_flight_at_end fields of historical session records.

decisions.title: Conversation field inventory, list-endpoint filters, master-pane Workstream column, soft-delete posture, eighteen acceptance criteria
decisions.context: Routine items batched together at the close of the conversation schema-design conversation: the field inventory beyond identifier/status/timestamps, the deliberately-excluded fields, the workstream-membership cardinality rule, the API surface, the master pane and detail pane layouts, soft-delete versus append-only posture, and the count and shape of testable acceptance criteria. Each item follows defaults from the schema-spec methodology guide; no significant deviations were warranted, but two natural additions warrant explicit decision recording (the list-endpoint filters for operational queries and the master-pane Workstream column for at-a-glance workstream membership).
decisions.decision: **(a) Field inventory** is six content/classification fields (conversation_identifier, conversation_title, conversation_purpose, conversation_description, conversation_notes, conversation_status) plus nine timestamps (three inherited base — created_at, updated_at, deleted_at; six per-status lifecycle per DEC-131 — kickoff_drafted_at, ready_at, started_at, completed_at, cancelled_at, superseded_at). Intended-deliverable description is folded into conversation_description rather than carried as a separate column. Deliberately excluded under minimum-viable scope: estimated_duration, priority, conversation_kind, outcome_summary — each is documented as future-release in spec section 3.8.3. **(b) Workstream membership** is mandatory at the access layer — every conversation has exactly one outgoing conversation_belongs_to_workstream edge; POSTs that omit the edge return HTTP 422 with {error: missing_workstream_membership_edge}; PATCH/PUT that removes the edge without replacing it returns the parallel 422. **(c) API surface** is the standard 8-endpoint set per spec guide section 3.5 plus two new query-string filters on the list endpoint: workstream_identifier filters by workstream membership; status filters by status value. The two filters compose. **(d) Master pane** has five columns (vs workstream's four), adding Workstream as the third column showing the workstream_identifier of the membership edge's target. Workstream-selector and status-selector combo boxes in the panel toolbar drive the two new query-string filters. Default sort by Identifier ascending. **(e) Detail pane** uses the default ListDetailPanel layout per spec guide section 3.6 with conditional lifecycle-timestamp rendering on the detail pane (showing only non-null timestamps) and complete-requires-session-edge / supersession-requires-edge UX (the user must add the matching outgoing edge before committing the status change). **(f) Soft-delete** posture is the default — soft-delete with restore — explicitly distinct from cancelled status (administrative housekeeping versus lifecycle outcome). **(g) Acceptance criteria** capture eighteen testable statements in spec section 3.7 (gate requires ten; workstream produced sixteen; two additional criteria added for the workstream-membership-required rule and the new list-endpoint filters).
decisions.rationale: Field inventory follows minimum-viable per the methodology guide; deferred fields are listed in spec section 3.8.3 with growth-condition language so future signal can justify additions. Workstream-membership-mandatory realizes DEC-120's binding text. List-endpoint filters are operational additions: 'what conversations are in workstream X' and 'what conversations are currently in_flight' are routine consultant questions, materially friction-reducing if the API answers directly rather than the client filtering an unfiltered list of 50+ conversations. Master-pane Workstream column is the at-a-glance complement — without it, the operator must open each conversation's detail to see its workstream. Soft-delete fits conversation because the entity is organizing metadata not transactional fact (append-only fits decisions, sessions, deposit_events; not conversation). Eighteen acceptance criteria fully satisfy the spec guide's per-spec completeness gate (section 7.1) and the cross-spec consistency check (section 7.2).
decisions.alternatives_considered: **Field inventory with conversation_kind enum** — rejected for minimum-viable scope; kind taxonomy emerges from real-use signal. **Workstream membership optional** — rejected because DEC-120's text binds. **API surface without list-endpoint filters** — would force client-side filtering of unfiltered lists; rejected because the filters are small additions with material query-time benefit. **Master pane without Workstream column** — would require detail-pane open to see workstream membership; rejected because workstream membership is the most operationally relevant property of a conversation. **Append-only posture for conversation** — rejected because conversation records can be created with the wrong title or pointed at the wrong workstream; correction via edit is appropriate. **Sixteen acceptance criteria (matching workstream)** — would have skipped the two additional rules-specific criteria; rejected because the workstream-membership-required rule and the list-endpoint filters are conversation-specific and deserve test coverage.
decisions.consequences: Conversations table has 15 columns (6 substantive + 3 base + 6 lifecycle per DEC-131). Two new query-string filter parameters on the list endpoint; test coverage required for both independently and composed. Master-pane Workstream column requires a small extension to ListDetailPanel's column-rendering (the column derives its value from a references-edge target rather than a direct column read); the build-planning conversation specifies the access-layer helper. Soft-delete is the default UI version 0.3 pattern with no code changes required. Eighteen acceptance criteria translate into specific test cases at build-planning time. The spec's per-spec completeness gate (spec guide section 7.1) passes: all nine subsections of section 3 substantively filled, eighteen acceptance criteria (gate requires ten), explicit category tags on open questions, cross-references include workstream plan, spec guide, workstream.md (the just-completed predecessor specification), foundation decisions DEC-117..122, and SES-048's six decisions DEC-123..128 as inherited precedents.
